name: MCP News Feed
description: News about MCP, AI agents, MCP servers, tool integrations, security, developer tooling, and enterprise AI adoption.

articles.title: I Built 3 MCP Servers for AI Agents — Here's How They Work
articles.summary: What are MCP Servers? The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that lets AI agents use external tools through a unified interface. Think of it as USB-C for AI — one protocol connects any AI client (Claude Desktop, Cursor, VS Code with Cline) to any tool or data source. I built three...
articles.author: Kenneth Doster

articles.title: The First MCP Server That Pays Your AI Agent
articles.summary: MCP (Model Context Protocol) lets AI assistants call external tools. Most MCP servers give your agent access to something — a database, a file system, a search engine. articles.title: The Signal Problem: Why your AI Agent needs Social Intelligence, not just Price Feeds
articles.summary: I've spent years building systems where the biggest bottleneck wasn't processing power or latency—it was noise. In crypto specifically, the noise is deafening. If you build an AI agent that only looks at price action and volume via a standard REST API, you're building a lagging indicator machine....

articles.title: The week in review: agents got wallets, rails, marketplaces and escrow. They still don't have settlement.
articles.summary: If you only tracked one part of the agent economy this June, you'd have missed how fast the rest of the stack is being built. articles.summary: Great point @alexshev — downloads ARE a vanity metric. The real signal is: did the agent connect, complete a workflow, and self-diagnose failures? articles.title: Your MCP database server needs a tool allowlist
articles.summary: Tool discovery is useful in a demo. In production, it can become a permission problem. If an MCP database server exposes every action an engineer might find useful, the agent has to choose safely every time. That is too much responsibility to put in a prompt. A safer pattern is an allowlist: expose...
articles.author: Mads Hansen

articles.title: Connecting ChatGPT to PostgreSQL? Start read-only.
articles.summary: The fastest way to make ChatGPT useful with PostgreSQL is also the easiest way to make production nervous. Someone connects a database, asks a natural-language question, and gets a useful answer. Then the next question is broader. Then the agent asks for raw rows. Then nobody is quite sure which...
articles.author: Mads Hansen

articles.title: Agent Runs now available in the Vercel MCP and CLI
articles.summary: Your agent can now inspect Agent Runs via the Vercel MCP and CLI for eve, the open-source agent framework.eve traces are automatically ingested when deployed to Vercel and available as Agent Runs. The new Vercel MCP tools and Vercel CLI commands let you find projects with runs, list recent runs,...
articles.author: Brooke Mosby

articles.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
articles.summary: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.
articles.author: Will Papper

articles.title: Beta SDKs for the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec Release Candidate Are Here
articles.summary: Beta releases of the Python, TypeScript, Go, and C# SDKs are now available with support for the 2026-07-28 MCP specification release candidate. Here is what changes for your server, how to migrate, and how to test before the spec goes final on July 28.

articles.title: MCP Server Authorization with Azure API Management: From Simple to Advanced
articles.summary: Why put API Management in front of your MCP servers The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has quickly become the standard way for AI agents, such as GitHub Copilot in VS Code, to reach external tools and data. articles.author: vzisiadis

articles.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
articles.summary: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider so users get connected servers on first login without per-app OAuth.
